brand.name: Mile Hi City Pickleball
brand.intro: Nine voices. One court. Every week, fresh stories from the players shaping pickleball culture in Colorado.

announcement.title: Our TikTok is up
announcement.body: Tag @milehicitypickleball as a collaborator on every post you put up so it lands on our feed too.

thisWeek.title: court summon jutsu
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): stand in the Mile Hi lobby in regular clothes, face camera straight on. Do a series of quick anime-style jutsu hand signs, cross your fingers, flip your wrists, two or three fast moves while looking completely serious. On the beat drop of 'Delirious' (the Food Jutsu TikTok sound), hard-cut: you are standing on Court 1 with your JOOLA paddle raised in full fit, the navy GAME ON wall directly behind you. Hold the pose two seconds. That is the whole video, under 15 seconds total. Search 'Delirious' or 'Food Jutsu' in TikTok sounds to find the audio. Caption: 'court summon jutsu · mile hi.' No voiceover, no B-roll, no script needed.
thisWeek.trendSource: Food Jutsu is the number-one TikTok trend of June 2026 per Epidemic Sound, with 36 million-plus posts. Creators do anime-style summoning hand signs and cut on a beat drop to reveal what they summoned. Celebrities including Idris Elba and Keke Palmer have posted versions. The 'Delirious' audio is the canonical sound. Sources: Know Your Meme (confirmed origin March 5, 2026), genz.ai, TikTok trending detail page. Adapts naturally to any venue reveal or outfit transformation.

thisWeek.title: you never take me here
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): look directly into camera with your most wronged, betrayed expression. 'Bangladesh' by Ian McConnell starts playing. Text on screen: 'you never take me to mile hi.' Beat 2: flip it. Cut to you walking through the Mile Hi front doors alone, picking up a JOOLA paddle at the desk, stepping onto Court 1. Final shot: you mid-rally with the navy GAME ON wall behind you, text: 'guess i had to take myself.' End text: 'mile hi : self date unlocked.' Under 30 seconds. Search 'Bangladesh Ian McConnell' in TikTok sounds. The format is the substitution: swap 'Bangladesh' for your personal unfulfilled ask and make it yours. Caption: 'took myself. it ate. no notes.'
thisWeek.trendSource: 'Bangladesh' by Ian McConnell is a 49-second track released June 5, 2026 that has gone viral on TikTok with SZA, Chance the Rapper, and Lizzo all engaging. The substitution format swaps 'Bangladesh' for any perpetually unfulfilled ask. Ian McConnell's Season 3 project drops July 10, renewing FYP attention this exact week. Sources: The Daily Star 2026, Yahoo Entertainment July 2026, TikTok #bangladeshsong tag.

thisWeek.title: confessions of a pickle addict
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): whisper-confessional close-up to camera, hands on cheeks, on-screen text: 'confessions of a mile hi regular.' Confession 1 with a straight face: 'I check if the courts are open before I check my messages.' Confession 2 with a guilty look: 'I have cried over a missed dink.' Confession 3 with a full grin: 'I told everyone it was just a phase. That was three months ago.' Final shot on Court 1, navy GAME ON wall behind you, text: 'the dinks are calling. i will not be taking questions.' 30 to 45 seconds, one cut per confession. Make it your personality: funny, honest, and obviously obsessed. Coach Jill walking by in the background of the final shot is a gold bonus if she is around. Caption: 'ok i have a problem · mile hi : court 1.'
thisWeek.trendSource: TikTok partnered with Madonna for the Confessions II album launch (July 3, 2026) with a global in-app campaign and real-world pop-ups, pushing confession-format content to every FYP this week. Honest relatable confession videos are a top-performing social proof format because they surface the viewer's own feeling about the sport and invite them to see themselves in it. Sources: TikTok Newsroom July 2026, Social Media Today 'TikTok partners with Madonna for Confessions II.' Both example URLs are TikTok discover pages.

thisWeek.title: mile hi full day pov
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): push open the Mile Hi front doors, camera pointed at you from inside as you walk in, wide eyes. Fast cuts only, 2 to 3 seconds per shot. Shot 2: grabbing your JOOLA paddle at the desk, give it a quick shake. Shot 3: first step onto Court 1, look down at the floor then up at the navy GAME ON wall. Shot 4: one rally, two or three dinks back and forth. Shot 5: you win a point, fist pump to camera. Shot 6: walking out, paddle tucked under your arm, grin directly to camera, text: 'see u tomorrow.' Under 30 seconds total. No voiceover needed. Use energetic trending audio from your TikTok library. If Coach Jill is around, sneak her in as your opponent on Shot 4. Gold bonus. Caption: 'mile hi pov : full day · same time tomorrow.'
thisWeek.trendSource: POV venue-day-in-life content drives local discovery for sports venues in 2026, with competitors like Sporty Pickle Denver using similar formats to convert scrollers into first-time visitors. Fast-cut POV under 30 seconds outperforms longer walkthroughs for venue accounts because it shows the full experience with zero friction. Sources: TikTok discover results for Sporty Pickle Denver + Feedspot Top 100 Pickleball TikTok Influencers 2026, July 2026.

trendRefs.label: Belle's picks · 4 trends to study this week
trendRefs.note: Open each one, watch a couple times, then make it yours. You can absolutely use the same format · the hook, the cuts, the pacing · just adapt it to our Mile Hi vibe and bring your own creativity and personality to it. The format is the base, your voice is what makes it land.

belleComments.intro: Hey guys! 💛 I'm really loving the performance and energy you're bringing this week. The personality, the effort, the storytelling, it's all showing up on screen and the squad is moving as one. Before you film the next round, peep the four TikTok refs at the top of this card · those are formats you can absolutely take and run with, just adapt them to Mile Hi and add your own creativity and personality on top. Two quick reminders below.

rules.title: 🔠 double-check your font before you export
rules.body: Quick eye on the text style every single time before you hit export. Are you on Quicksand Bold lowercase for the main caption + Anton ALL CAPS for the punch label, or did CapCut default back to its system font? Defaults make the feed look random and break the squad look. The full font recipe lives in section 07 'before you post' at the bottom of this portal · open it before every edit. Same fonts every video so the grid reads as one team.

rules.title: 📱 don't forget to tag us on TikTok
rules.body: Every single TikTok you post needs to tag @milehicitypickleball in the caption (and mention us in the video if it fits naturally). I've seen a couple of posts go up without the tag · please don't forget. TikTok uses tags to connect content across accounts, so when ours is missing, the algorithm doesn't link you to our page. Tag us every time, no exceptions.

belleComments.outro: Keep putting effort, personality, and creativity into it · that's exactly what Mile Hi needs. The earlier coaching notes (mic distance, captions, IG collaborator) still apply too · scroll down to refresh anytime. Thank you guys so much! If you have any questions, you have my number and Tricia's too. 💛

belleComments.intro: Hey guys! 💛 I'm reviewing the videos and I just wanted to say great job. I can really see the progress, the effort, and the creativity you're putting in. Thank you for paying attention to the notes, it really shows. Also, what are you guys thinking about the page? Are you watching what's being posted? I'd love to know what you think.

rules.title: 🎤 mic distance · four fingers off your mouth
rules.body: When you're holding the mic, keep about four fingers of space between the mic and your mouth. The audio comes out cleaner and way easier to understand on playback. Too close and it muffles, too far and it loses you.

rules.title: ✍️ captions · white text + black outline
rules.body: Saw most of you nailing this last round, keep it going. A couple of you had only white or only black, which is totally okay while we're learning, but let's lock the format in moving forward: white letters with a black outline / stroke. Same every video so the feed reads as one squad.

rules.title: 🤝 IG · tag mile hi as a collaborator
rules.body: When you post your Mile Hi content on Instagram, don't just @ the account · use the COLLABORATOR option. When you upload your post, tap Tag People, scroll down and tap Invite Collaborator, then add @milehicitypickleball. Once Tricia approves on her end, your post shows on YOUR feed AND on Mile Hi's feed at the same time. Same post, double the reach, zero extra work. This is how we grow both accounts together.

belleComments.outro: Besides that, I'm really happy with what I'm seeing. Keep putting effort, personality, and creativity into it. That's exactly what Mile Hi needs. Thank you guys so much! If you have any questions, you have my number and Tricia's too. 💛

belleComments.intro: Good job, squad! Videos are looking strong this week. Real love for the music sync on the edits, the storytelling about coming into Mile Hi (showing the doors, the desk, the courts), and the focus on what the venue actually feels like. That's the squad voice landing. A few small brand things to lock in across all 9 of you so the grid feels like ONE roster instead of nine different accounts. These aren't call-outs · just patterns I noticed this week that we can all level up on.

rules.title: captions · same look every time
rules.body: When everyone uses a different font, the feed looks off. Let's all use white text with a black outline so the squad reads as one team. The full recipe lives in section 07 'before you post' at the bottom of this portal. Peek at it before you edit.

rules.title: pov format · keep it the same
rules.body: Always go with 'mile hi : POV' · logo, then colon, then POV in all caps. Same format every video. Makes us instantly recognizable when someone scrolls past.

rules.title: caption position · center it
rules.body: Drop your caption right in the middle. If you can't center, nudge it just above the middle. The bottom of the screen is where the TikTok UI sits, so text down there gets covered up and nobody reads it.

rules.title: always have a caption
rules.body: Every video gets text on screen. Yes, even the dance ones (especially the dance ones). The caption is what makes you searchable AND what holds viewers who aren't already into pickleball.

rules.title: use the mile hi joola paddle
rules.body: Grab a Mile Hi JOOLA paddle before you film. Your own paddle, a Monarch, a friend's, those don't read as Mile Hi when someone scrolls past. The brand paddle IS the brand signal.

rules.title: keep it in color
rules.body: Mile Hi is bright. The court is orange and navy, the vibe is energy. Please skip black-and-white filters and moody desaturation. Color always.

rules.title: music · keep the energy up
rules.body: Mile Hi is energy + community, so default to songs that lift the room. Exception · if a trend card at the top of the portal specifically calls for a slower or moody song (it'll happen sometimes), that's fine, follow the trend. Otherwise, if a song makes YOU feel sluggish, it'll make your viewer scroll.

rules.title: follow the trend cards at the top
rules.body: Sometimes a trend will feel a little weird and that's okay. We pick them on purpose · they're part of our strategy and the data shows what's going to work this week. Trust the cards even when they don't make sense in the moment.

belleComments.outro: For everyone just starting · you're doing GREAT. These are small tweaks, not rewrites. Pick the 2 easiest for you and lock them in this week. The squad that iterates wins.

images.label: image 1
images.caption: Wrong paddle (Monarch). Use the Mile Hi JOOLA paddle. The brand paddle IS the signal on the FYP.

images.label: image 2
images.caption: Caption parked at the bottom. Pull it to center or slightly above. Nobody reads the lower third.

images.label: image 3
images.caption: Caption blends into the background. Switch to white text with a black outline so it pops on any shot.

images.label: image 4
images.caption: Story shot with no caption. Every clip needs text on screen, even the establishing ones. Tell the viewer what they're looking at in 2 seconds.

images.label: image 5
images.caption: Dance shot with no paddles in hand AND the logo on the wall is mirrored (filmed front-facing without flipping back). Keep paddles in the frame on dance trends, and un-mirror your front-camera clips.

images.label: image 6
images.caption: POV format is off. Use 'mile hi : POV' (logo, then colon, then POV in all caps). Lock this format every time.

images.label: image 7
images.caption: Black-and-white / desaturated filter. Mile Hi is color + court + energy. No grayscale, no moody.

images.label: image 8
images.caption: POV without the colon + the logo prefix. Same fix as image 6 · 'mile hi : POV' is the format.

images.label: image 9
images.caption: Caption font doesn't match the squad recipe. Quicksand Bold lowercase + Anton caps for the punch word. Same across everyone, every video.

images.label: image 10
images.caption: Caption at the bottom AND overlapping the orange court line. Hard to read. Center the text or push it slightly above middle.

belleComments.comment: Organize your Drive folder, every time. RAW = everything straight off your phone (even the takes you don't love). EDITED = your final cut, ready to post. We pull from EDITED only · if it's not there, it doesn't get posted. Takes 30 seconds to drag, saves everyone hours.

When you film the dance or lip-sync trends, ALWAYS keep the paddle in your hands. Even mid-spin, mid-twirl, mid-beat-drop. The paddle is the brand signal that makes us instantly recognizable on the FYP. Without it, the video could be from any sport, so please keep it on you the whole time.

weeklyReport.summary: The squad holds 54 edited clips in Drive across 7 of 9 creators, but no TikTok posts have been logged yet. This is the week to go live. Four new trends for Jul 6 · Jul 12: a Food Jutsu court-reveal transformation for all 9, a Bangladesh sound adaptation for two creators, a Confessions of a Pickle Addict social proof for three, and a fast-cut full-day POV for four.

topCreator.name: Annie Turner
topCreator.reason: Fourteen edited clips in Drive, the highest on the squad, with new uploads added as recently as July 4.

watchOuts: Two creators have zero edited videos in their Drive folder. If that is you, your one job this week is to film, edit, and upload one clip. An empty folder means nothing goes live for you.

Several clips added this week are sitting as drafts with no caption or hashtags. If that is you, open your portal card and drop the caption before you post. Tricia pulls from that field, so a blank means it goes out unbranded.
weeklyReport.production: 67 edited videos across the squad this week. Top of production at 14 clips, median at 9.
